pi_items.metadata_value: Multiply blend mode darkens underlying image
pi_items.verified_value: Orange overlay with multiply blend darkens image by 55-62% depending on base colors
pi_items.source: Test with RGB multiply formula: sky blue (135,206,235) becomes (135,82,0); forest green (34,139,34) becomes (34,55,0); brightness reduced from 192 to 72.5 and 69 to 30

verifier.py line 150; README states 15-40% but code checks 15-60%

privileged_info_summary: The blend_mode_multiply task uses real Unsplash landscape images (800x533 and 800x450 pixels) plus a synthetic orange (#FF6600) overlay at 800x600 with 70% alpha. The task requires changing a layer blend mode from Normal to Multiply. The standard multiply formula (base*overlay)/255 darkens typical landscape images by 55-62%. GIMP assigns blend mode ID 3 to Multiply. The verifier uses gimpformats to parse XCF files and checks that the top layer has blend mode = 3, with visual validation using SSIM comparison (code checks >= 0.85, not >= 0.90 as documented) and brightness reduction checking (code allows 15-60%, not 15-40% as documented).
